message.content:
I'm planning a refactor in the Recipe Bottle codebase (working dir /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster). I need a precise inventory of how the theurge test suite "fast" cases bypass tabtargets and reach into module internals, so I can plan migrating them to contract surfaces.

The primary file is `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrf_fast.rs`. Sibling files possibly with the same pattern: `rbtdrk_canonical.rs`, `rbtdro_onboarding.rs`, `rbtdrp_pristine.rs`, `rbtdrf_handbook.rs` (all in `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/`).

Please report (with file:line references):

1. In `rbtdrf_fast.rs`: how many test cases exist, grouped by family. Specifically the enrollment-validation family and regime-validation family. Also find the two "all-*" cases `rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels` and `rbtdrf_rv_rbrn_all_nameplates`.

2. The "bypass" pattern: show me concrete examples of where the tests:
   - Encode module source paths as string literals (e.g. `Tools/rbk/rbrr_regime.sh`) inside `format!` blocks — list which module paths are hardcoded and roughly how many sites.
   - Replicate kindle chains (source-and-kindle ordering, calls to `z*_kindle` functions).
   - Call private `z`-prefixed functions directly.

3. The engine helpers: find `rbtdrf_run_ev`, `rbtdrf_run_rv`, `rbtdrf_run_bash`, and any `rbtdrf_run_tt` / `rbtdrf_run_tt_neg` (these last two may have been minted recently in a pace coded ₢BBABJ). Show their signatures and what they do — particularly whether a tabtarget-invocation helper already exists.

4. For each sibling file (rbtdrk_canonical.rs, rbtdro_onboarding.rs, rbtdrp_pristine.rs, rbtdrf_handbook.rs): does it use the same bypass pattern (hardcoded paths / direct z-calls) or does it already go through tabtargets? Give me a one-line characterization of each with evidence.

5. Categorize the enrollment-validation and regime-validation cases by what they actually test: is there an existing operator tabtarget that covers the operation (e.g. rbw-rnv ValidateNameplateRegime, rbw-rvv ValidateVesselRegime, etc.)? Map the test families to candidate tabtargets if you can identify them.

6. Find `rbtdri_invocation.rs` lines ~19-20 — there's a documented principle about "theurge invokes bottle operations exclusively through tabtargets". Show it.

Be thorough — read excerpts and give me file:line locations with actual code snippets. I need the conclusion (what the bypass looks like, how many sites, what's already migratable), not whole-file dumps.

31	    RBTDRM_FIXTURE_DOCKERFILE_HYGIENE, RBTDRM_FIXTURE_ENROLLMENT_VALIDATION,
32	    RBTDRM_FIXTURE_REGIME_SMOKE, RBTDRM_FIXTURE_REGIME_VALIDATION,

34	use crate::rbtdrx_platform::rbtdrx_native_to_posix;

36	// ── Helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
37	
38	/// Sub-assertion within a case: run bash snippet, check exit code.
39	struct RbtdrfSub {
40	    label: &'static str,
41	    setup: &'static str,
42	    command: &'static str,
43	    expect_ok: bool,
44	}
45	
46	impl RbtdrfSub {
47	    const fn ok(label: &'static str, setup: &'static str) -> Self {
48	        Self { label, setup, command: "buv_vet \"TEST\"", expect_ok: true }
49	    }
50	    const fn fatal(label: &'static str, setup: &'static str) -> Self {
51	        Self { label, setup, command: "buv_vet \"TEST\"", expect_ok: false }
52	    }
53	    const fn ok_cmd(label: &'static str, setup: &'static str, command: &'static str) -> Self {
54	        Self { label, setup, command, expect_ok: true }
55	    }
56	    const fn fatal_cmd(label: &'static str, setup: &'static str, command: &'static str) -> Self {
57	        Self { label, setup, command, expect_ok: false }
58	    }
59	}
60	
61	/// Run a bash script, return (exit_code, stdout, stderr). Saves traces to case dir.
62	fn rbtdrf_run_bash(
63	    project_root: &Path,
64	    script: &str,
65	    dir: &Path,
66	    trace_prefix: &str,
67	) -> Result<(i32, String, String), String> {
68	    let output = Command::new("bash")
69	        .arg("-c")
70	        .arg(script)
71	        .current_dir(project_root)
72	        .output()
73	        .map_err(|e| format!("bash execution failed: {}", e))?;
74	
75	    let stdout = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout).into_owned();
76	    let stderr = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr).into_owned();
77	    let code = output.status.code().unwrap_or(-1);
78	
79	    let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join(format!("{}-script.sh", trace_prefix)), script);
80	    let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join(format!("{}-stdout.txt", trace_prefix)), &stdout);
81	    let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join(format!("{}-stderr.txt", trace_prefix)), &stderr);
82	
83	    Ok((code, stdout, stderr))
84	}
85	
86	/// Run enrollment-validation sub-assertions against BUV.
87	fn rbtdrf_run_ev(
88	    dir: &Path,
89	    enrollment: &str,
90	    subs: &[RbtdrfSub],
91	) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
92	    let root = match std::env::current_dir() {
93	        Ok(r) => r,
94	        Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("cannot get cwd: {}", e)),
95	    };
96	    let buv = root.join("Tools/buk/buv_validation.sh");
97	
98	    for (i, sub) in subs.iter().enumerate() {
99	        let script = format!(
100	            "set -euo pipefail\nsource '{}'\nzbuv_kindle\nzbuv_reset_enrollment\n{}\n{}\n{}",
101	            rbtdrx_native_to_posix(&buv),
102	            enrollment,
103	            sub.setup,
104	            sub.command,
105	        );
106	
107	        match rbtdrf_run_bash(&root, &script, dir, &format!("sub-{}", i)) {
108	            Ok((code, _, _)) => {
109	                let ok = code == 0;
110	                if ok != sub.expect_ok {
111	                    return if sub.expect_ok {
112	                        rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("{}: expected ok, got exit {}", sub.label, code))
113	                    } else {
114	                        rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("{}: expected failure, got exit 0", sub.label))
115	                    };
116	                }
117	            }
118	            Err(e) => {
119	                return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("{}: {}", sub.label, e));
120	            }
121	        }
122	    }
123	    rbtdre_Verdict::Pass
124	}
125	
126	/// Run a regime-validation case: source modules, set up state, kindle+enforce.
127	fn rbtdrf_run_rv(
128	    dir: &Path,
129	    preamble: &str,
130	    setup: &str,
131	    expect_ok: bool,
132	    label: &str,
133	) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
134	    let root = match std::env::current_dir() {
135	        Ok(r) => r,
136	        Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("cannot get cwd: {}", e)),
137	    };
138	    let buv = root.join("Tools/buk/buv_validation.sh");
139	    let rbk = root.join("Tools/rbk");
140	
141	    let buv_p = rbtdrx_native_to_posix(&buv);
142	    let rbk_p = rbtdrx_native_to_posix(&rbk);
143	    let script = format!(
144	        "set -euo pipefail\n\
145	         source '{}'\n\
146	         source '{}/rbcc_Constants.sh'\n\
147	         source '{}/rbgc_Constants.sh'\n\
148	         source '{}/rbrr_regime.sh'\n\
149	         source '{}/rbrd_regime.sh'\n\
150	         source '{}/rbrv_regime.sh'\n\
151	         source '{}/rbrn_regime.sh'\n\
152	         source '{}/rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh'\n\
153	         zbuv_kindle\nzrbcc_kindle\n\
154	         {}\n\
155	         {}",
156	        buv_p,
157	        rbk_p, rbk_p, rbk_p,
158	        rbk_p, rbk_p, rbk_p, rbk_p,
159	        preamble, setup,
160	    );
161	
162	    match rbtdrf_run_bash(&root, &script, dir, label) {
163	        Ok((code, _, _)) => {
164	            let ok = code == 0;
165	            if ok != expect_ok {
166	                return if expect_ok {
167	                    rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("{}: expected ok, got exit {}", label, code))
168	                } else {
169	                    rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("{}: expected failure, got exit 0", label))
170	                };
171	            }
172	            rbtdre_Verdict::Pass
173	        }
174	        Err(e) => rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("{}: {}", label, e)),
175	    }
176	}

178	/// Run a tabtarget and check exit 0.
179	fn rbtdrf_run_tt(
180	    project_root: &Path,
181	    colophon: &str,
182	    args: &[&str],
183	    dir: &Path,
184	    label: &str,
185	) -> Result<(), String> {
186	    let tt = rbtdri_find_tabtarget_global(project_root, colophon)?;
187	    let output = Command::new(&tt)
188	        .args(args)
189	        .current_dir(project_root)
190	        .output()
191	        .map_err(|e| format!("{}: failed to run {}: {}", label, tt.display(), e))?;
192	
193	    let code = output.status.code().unwrap_or(-1);
194	    let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join(format!("{}-stdout.txt", label)), &output.stdout);
195	    let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join(format!("{}-stderr.txt", label)), &output.stderr);
196	
197	    if code != 0 {
198	        return Err(format!(
199	            "{}: {} exited {} — {}",
200	            label,
201	            colophon,
202	            code,
203	            String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr),
204	        ));
205	    }
206	    Ok(())
207	}

209	/// Run a tabtarget that is expected to fail. Inverts rbtdrf_run_tt:
210	/// non-zero exit returns Ok(()), zero exit returns Err. Invocation errors
211	/// (tabtarget not found, launcher failure) still propagate as Err.
212	fn rbtdrf_run_tt_neg(
213	    project_root: &Path,
214	    colophon: &str,

216	    dir: &Path,
217	    label: &str,
218	) -> Result<(), String> {

220	    let output = Command::new(&tt)
221	        .args(args)
222	        .current_dir(project_root)
223	        .output()
224	        .map_err(|e| format!("{}: failed to run {}: {}", label, tt.display(), e))?;
225	
226	    let code = output.status.code().unwrap_or(-1);
227	    let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join(format!("{}-stdout.txt", label)), &output.stdout);
228	    let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join(format!("{}-stderr.txt", label)), &output.stderr);
229	
230	    if code == 0 {
231	        return Err(format!(
232	            "{}: {} expected failure, got exit 0",
233	            label, colophon,
234	        ));
235	    }
236	    Ok(())
237	}

494	
495	const RBTDRF_VALID_DIGEST: &str =
496	    "sha256:abcdef0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef0123456789";
497	
498	fn rbtdrf_ev_odref_valid(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
499	    let root = match std::env::current_dir() {
500	        Ok(r) => r,
501	        Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("cannot get cwd: {}", e)),
502	    };
503	    let buv = root.join("Tools/buk/buv_validation.sh");
504	    let enrollment = format!(
505	        "set -euo pipefail\nsource '{}'\nzbuv_kindle\nzbuv_reset_enrollment\n\
506	         buv_regime_enroll \"TEST\"\nbuv_group_enroll \"References\"\n\
507	         buv_odref_enroll TEST_IMAGE \"Container image\"",
508	        rbtdrx_native_to_posix(&buv),
509	    );
510	    let d = RBTDRF_VALID_DIGEST;
511	
512	    let subs: &[(&str, &str)] = &[
513	        ("standard registry", &format!("docker.io/library/alpine@{}", d)),
514	        ("multi-level repo", &format!("us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/my-proj/my-repo/tool@{}", d)),
515	        ("registry with port", &format!("registry.local:5000/myimage@{}", d)),
516	    ];
517	
518	    for (i, (label, image)) in subs.iter().enumerate() {
519	        let script = format!("{}\nexport TEST_IMAGE=\"{}\"\nbuv_vet \"TEST\"", enrollment, image);
520	        match rbtdrf_run_bash(&root, &script, dir, &format!("sub-{}", i)) {
521	            Ok((0, _, _)) => {}
522	            Ok((code, _, _)) => {
523	                return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("{}: expected ok, got exit {}", label, code));
524	            }
525	            Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("{}: {}", label, e)),
526	        }
527	    }
528	    rbtdre_Verdict::Pass
529	}

761	fn rbtdrf_ev_multiscope(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
762	    let root = match std::env::current_dir() {
763	        Ok(r) => r,
764	        Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("cannot get cwd: {}", e)),
765	    };
766	    let buv = root.join("Tools/buk/buv_validation.sh");
767	
768	    // Two-scope enrollment: ALPHA and BETA
769	    let enrollment = format!(
770	        "set -euo pipefail\nsource '{}'\nzbuv_kindle\nzbuv_reset_enrollment\n\
771	         buv_regime_enroll \"ALPHA\"\nbuv_group_enroll \"Alpha Vars\"\n\
772	         buv_bool_enroll TEST_ALPHA_FLAG \"Alpha flag\"\n\
773	         buv_regime_enroll \"BETA\"\nbuv_group_enroll \"Beta Vars\"\n\
774	         buv_bool_enroll TEST_BETA_FLAG \"Beta flag\"\n\
775	         export TEST_ALPHA_FLAG=\"1\"\nexport TEST_BETA_FLAG=\"bad\"\n",
776	        rbtdrx_native_to_posix(&buv),
777	    );
778	
779	    // Sub 1: vet ALPHA passes (BETA is bad but not in scope)
780	    let script1 = format!("{}buv_vet \"ALPHA\"", enrollment);
781	    match rbtdrf_run_bash(&root, &script1, dir, "sub-0") {
782	        Ok((code, _, _)) if code == 0 => {}
783	        Ok((code, _, _)) => {
784	            return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("alpha scope: expected ok, got exit {}", code));
785	        }
786	        Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("alpha scope: {}", e)),
787	    }
788	
789	    // Sub 2: vet BETA fails
790	    let script2 = format!("{}buv_vet \"BETA\"", enrollment);
791	    match rbtdrf_run_bash(&root, &script2, dir, "sub-1") {
792	        Ok((code, _, _)) if code != 0 => {}
793	        Ok((_, _, _)) => {
794	            return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail("beta scope: expected failure, got exit 0".to_string());
795	        }
796	        Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("beta scope: {}", e)),
797	    }
798	
799	    rbtdre_Verdict::Pass
800	}
